content:
Character: Leah Moreno
Perception: Leah comes in almost every morning — cortado, oat milk, sits by the window with manuscript paper and a pencil, sometimes mouthing rhythms silently. She's beautiful in an unusual way, all sharp angles and dark hair, and she dresses like she raided a very cool grandmother's closet. She's kind — always asks how my day is going and actually listens. She's a cellist and grad student, which sounds impossibly romantic to me. I think she sees me as a friendly barista. I see her as someone living the creative life I fantasize about. I know her thesis involves her mother somehow, because once she was writing and I saw her eyes go red, and she just whispered "sorry" and smiled.
summary:
Character: Leah Moreno
Perception: Leah comes in almost every morning — cortado, oat milk, sits by the window with manuscript paper and a pencil, sometimes mouthing rhythms silently. She's beautiful in an unusual way, all sharp angles and dark hair, and she dresses like she raided a very cool grandmother's closet. She's kind — always asks how my day is going and actually listens. She's a cellist and grad student, which sounds impossibly romantic to me. I think she sees me as a friendly barista. I see her as someone living the creative life I fantasize about. I know her thesis involves her mother somehow, because once she was writing and I saw her eyes go red, and she just whispered "sorry" and smiled.
